Fix neo4j container startup failure caused by read-only volume mount

Neo4j 5 chowns everything under /var/lib/neo4j/ on startup, which
fails on the :ro mounted init-graph.cypher. The file is already
mounted separately in the neo4j-init sidecar container.

docs: restructure README with logical narrative flow

Reorder sections to tell a coherent story:
1. Vulnerability overview
2. Root cause analysis (vulnerable code)
3. Patch code analysis (fix)
4. Official patch test code (blueprint for this lab)
5. Lab implementation (derived from patch test)
6. Attack details + curl PoC
7. Setup, usage, and results

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Implement smart quality ranking system for dashboard variety and quality

User reported seeing repetitive, low-quality dashboards on app launch. Root causes:
1. Binary pass/fail quality filter - no ranking by engagement metrics
2. No category boosting - all categories treated equally
3. Small initial load (20 dashboards) - insufficient variety
4. Limited Tableau search diversity

Solution - Smart Quality Scoring System:

1. Quality Scoring Functions (useDashboards.ts):
   - meetsMinimumQuality(): Hard requirements filter (thumbnail, description, title)
   - calculateQualityScore(): Scores 10-80 based on viewCount, favoriteCount, engagement ratio
   - getCategoryBoost(): +25 points for Health/Sports/Marketing when viewing "all categories"
   - getFinalScore(): Combined quality + category boost
   - ensureSourceDiversity(): Round-robin platform balancing to prevent single source dominance

2. Weighted Shuffle Algorithm (shuffleArray.ts):
   - New weightedShuffle() function maintains randomness while favoring quality
   - Breaks dashboards into tiers: 60% top (score 60+), 30% mid (40-60), 10% baseline (20-40)
   - Interleaves tiers with 70%/25%/5% probability for serendipity
   - Ensures high-quality dashboards appear earlier without pure sorting

3. Enhanced Fetch Logic:
   - Increased DASHBOARDS_PER_LOAD from 20 to 45 (2.25x more variety)
   - Replaced binary filter with quality scoring + weighted shuffle
   - Added source diversity balancing after shuffle

4. Tableau Search Expansion:
   - Expanded SEARCH_TERMS from 45 to 69 terms
   - Added 12 health-focused terms (healthcare dashboard, patient care, medical data, etc.)
   - Added 12 sports-focused terms (NBA statistics, cricket analytics, Olympics, etc.)
   - Added 12 marketing-focused terms (campaign dashboard, social media analytics, etc.)
   - Maintains existing high-quality general terms

Expected Results:
- Initial load: 45 dashboards (up from 20)
- Quality distribution: 60% high-quality (popular), 30% medium, 10% baseline
- Category distribution when viewing "all categories": 25-30% Health/Sports/Marketing each
- Source distribution: 50-60% Tableau (variety), 25-35% NovyPro (curated), 10-20% PowerBI
- No single platform dominates feed
- Maintains randomness while prioritizing engagement and user preferences

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

commit.message:
Fire transcription per chunk instead of concatenating audio

Each voice.wav chunk now triggers an async transcription immediately.
When voice_last.wav arrives, all pending results are collected in order
and joined as text. Removes ffmpeg concat dependency — transcription
runs in parallel with recording for lower latency.

commit.message:
feat(orchestrator): plan-scoped dirs, hook stripping, LLM response caching (#98)

* feat(orchestrator): plan-scoped dirs, hook stripping, LLM response caching

- Fix normalizeOutput to strip plugin hookSpecificOutput blocks using
  brace-depth matching instead of fragile regex — prevents planner crash
  when superpowers plugin injects context into spawned CLI stdout
- Scope plan directories to .frankenbeast/plans/<plan-name>/ to prevent
  plan collisions; derive plan name from design doc filename
- Cache raw LLM decomposition response to llm-response.json for debugging
- Add --plan-name CLI flag for explicit plan naming
- Add generatePlanName() utility exported from package index
- 8 new normalizeOutput tests, 4 generatePlanName tests, 2 plan-scoped path tests

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* chore: add eslint flat configs for all packages with lint scripts

Packages had lint scripts but no eslint.config.js, causing CI failure
with ESLint 10. Adds flat configs matching the existing pattern from
franken-planner/franken-critique. Pre-existing violations set to warn
to avoid fixing dozens of unrelated files in this PR. Also removes
stale eslint-disable directives in orchestrator source.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(orchestrator): guard main() in run.ts to prevent import-time execution

main() was called unconditionally at module level, causing process.exit(1)
when tests imported run.ts for resolvePhases/createStdinIO. Guard with
import.meta.url check so main() only runs as CLI entry point. Export main()
and update tests to call it explicitly.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Prevent OOM crashes with memory-gated sync and swap (#52)

* fix: memory-gated LLM stop/start during sync + swap + sudoers

- Sync pipeline checks GPU memory before embedding phases; only stops
  llama-server if insufficient. Restores pre-sync state afterward.
- Adds lifeos-llm to sudoers for passwordless stop/start from sync.
- Creates 8 GB swap file as OOM safety net.
- Documents BIOS allocation recommendation and cgroup setup.
- Suppresses E402 in scripts/ (intentional early load_dotenv).
- Fixes pre-existing lint: unused import, unused vars, bare f-string.

Closes #50

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix: address PR review — crash safety, skip-guard ordering, tests

- Wrap sync loop in try/finally so LLM restart fires on any exit path
- Add LLM restart to SIGTERM handler via module-level globals
- Move memory-gating check after skip guards (disabled, monthly,
  recently-synced, dependency-failed) to avoid needless LLM stop
- Use enumerate() instead of sources.index() for O(1) lookup
- Fix sudoers Runas spec: ALL=(root) instead of ALL=(ALL)
- Fix log message: show "unknown" instead of "None MB"
- Add 7 tests for LLM memory gating (stop/start/skip/safety-net)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: separate risk_score from coverage to stop false high-risk verdicts

ROOT CAUSE: Missing/unimplemented signals (social, developer_code) earned 0
points but still counted toward the 100-point denominator. This permanently
capped scores at ~65-75 even for legitimate tokens, forcing false HIGH_RISK
verdicts on established projects like JUP and BONK.

SCORING ENGINE (packages/common/scoring.py):
- Add risk_score: normalized to ASSESSED categories only (not 100)
- Add coverage: fraction of max_points that had actual data (0.0-1.0)
- Add assessed_max: how many points were actually assessable
- Add has_data flag per CategoryScore
- Verdict now uses risk_score + coverage:
  - Low coverage (<30%) caps at SUSPICIOUS (refuse to condemn on thin evidence)
  - Normal bands: >=75 Legit, >=55 Suspicious, >=30 High Risk, <30 Larp
- Fix: removed deployer_reputation from capital_lineage_quality (was double-counted)
- Fix: added narrative_pump_signal to cross_signal_consistency category

SIGNAL CALCULATION (workers/fetch/investigate.py):
- Fix: bags_launched signal only emitted for actual Bags launches
  (non-Bags tokens no longer penalized with 0.0)
- Fix: deployer_reputation depth=0 now neutral (0.5) not lean-negative (0.4)
- Fix: capital_origin_score when funding untraceable now neutral (0.5) not 0.3
- Report now exposes: risk_score, coverage, no_data_categories, assessed_max
- Executive summary includes coverage % and missing category explanation

BEFORE vs AFTER (live benchmarks):
  JUP:  41.48/HIGH_RISK  ->  69.03/SUSPICIOUS  (75% coverage)
  BONK: 45.81/HIGH_RISK  ->  64.39/SUSPICIOUS  (75% coverage)
  RAY:  ~33/LARP         ->  50.55/HIGH_RISK    (65% coverage)

Design rules enforced:
- Missing data != bad data
- Scoring and evidence coverage are now separated
- Reports explain why confidence is low when data sources fail
- Bad tokens still correctly flagged (rug simulation: 14.67/LARP)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

ENV-001: Add pure environmental adjustment library (lib/envAdjust.ts) (#22)

* ENV-001: Add pure environmental adjustment library (lib/envAdjust.ts)

Implements all 6 exported functions and the EnvNormalizationResult interface
for temperature/humidity/altitude normalization of Efficiency Factor values:
computeHumidityPartialPressure (August-Roche-Magnus), computeEffectiveTemperature
(Steadman 1979), computePerformanceFactor (15°C baseline, 0.5 floor),
computeAltitudeFactor (>1500m correction), normalizeTempEF (full correction
pipeline), and fitHeatSensitivityK (OLS regression, min 30 runs + 15°C range).

Adds 29 unit tests in __tests__/envAdjust.test.ts; all pass including boundary
conditions, clamping, null humidity/elevation handling, and the OLS k-fit.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* ENV-001: Add envAdjust.test.ts to npm test script

commit.message:
feat: redesign .kno project binding, streamline onboarding, default to active nudges

Replace auto_load_on_confirm with declarative page binding in .kno files.
A `page = "name"` entry now auto-loads the bound page on /kno.start —
no confirmation needed. The .kno concept is general (any directory), with
git repos getting prompted to create one.

Collapse the first-save onboarding chain from 6 confirmations to 2:
capture confirm + page confirm. In git context, one "yes" to the page
offer creates the page, curates the note in, and binds .kno for auto-load.

Change default nudge_level from "light" to "active" so new users see
value in their first session. Light mode is repositioned for power users
who know the tool and prefer minimal interruption.

Key changes:
- RepoConfig: add Page field, remove AutoLoadOnConfirm
- config: add prompt_project_setup vault setting
- DetectSessionContext: check cwd for .kno even without .git
- kno_set_option: route project keys to .kno, vault keys to config.toml
- vault_status: emit project.page instead of skill.auto_load_on_confirm
- Skills: streamlined start, capture, page, awareness instructions
- Docs: .kno moved to user guide, dev guide scoped to dev-only features

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
